meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge with this beginner-friendly checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first project.
content.answer_block: SaaS pricing is not about guessing; it is about value-based alignment. By mapping feature tiers to specific user pain points and integrating Stripe via Antigravity, you transform code into a sustainable business. Use Claude Code to automate the implementation, ensuring your first project is professional, scalable, and revenue-ready.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking in rhythm with your anxiety. You have the code, but you have no SaaS knowledge—no strategy to turn lines of logic into a sustainable digital engine. The gap between a student project and a real-world product feels like an abyss, and the hardest part isn't the syntax; it's the monetization strategy.

But the fog is lifting. Today, we bridge that gap. We will turn your fear into a competitive advantage by mastering the art of SaaS pricing. With the raw power of Antigravity and the intelligent orchestration of Claude Code, you are about to move from 'just a student' to a serious AI builder.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the unit of value your user pays for (e.g., tokens, storage, or seats). This prevents 'feature-bloat' pricing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project codebase and suggest three value-based pricing tiers based on the core functionality, focusing on usage-based versus flat-rate models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Analyze codebase for value metrics and draft a pricing table schema'.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Structure your database to support multiple tiers, ensuring your backend can handle feature flagging for different user levels.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application that supports subscription tiers and feature-gating.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file schema.prisma --instruction 'Implement a subscription model with user roles and tier-based feature access'.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Payment Flow
steps.description: Connect your application to Stripe to handle secure checkout sessions and subscription lifecycle management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure checkout session handler using the Stripe Node.js SDK, including webhooks for subscription status updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Setup Stripe checkout flow and webhook listener in the backend service'.

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Build a middleware layer that checks the user's subscription status before allowing access to premium features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware function that validates user subscription status against the database before executing API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file middleware.ts --instruction 'Create a gatekeeper function for premium API endpoints'.

steps.title: Analytics and Conversion Tracking
steps.description: Add basic tracking to see which pricing tier your users are clicking on most frequently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a lightweight analytics hook that logs pricing tier selection events to an external dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate conversion tracking for pricing page clicks'.

pro_tips: Always start with a 'Free Tier' that provides just enough value to solve one specific problem, creating a 'hook' for your users.
Never hardcode your prices; use a remote configuration service so you can adjust pricing without redeploying your entire application.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your webhooks to avoid immediate service cutoff during failed payment retries.
Offer an annual discount that is at least 20% cheaper than monthly; it improves cash flow and reduces churn significantly.
Use 'Feature Signaling' on your UI to show locked items, which acts as a constant, subtle reminder of the value available in paid tiers.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to a staging environment, run a mock payment test using Stripe test cards, and gather feedback from at least five peers to validate your pricing logic.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to transform your code into a professional SaaS product. Don't let your project sit on GitHub gathering dust—it's time to build, launch, and scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
